# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create an Istio PeerAuthentication policy for our microservices namespace. Configure mTLS mode to allow both plaintext and encrypted traffic for backward compatibility. This is for our Kubernetes cluster during migration to service mesh.
# Category: istio_permissive_mtls

apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: PeerAuthentication
metadata:
  name: microservices-mtls-permissive
  namespace: microservices
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: microservices-mtls-policy
    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: service-mesh
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: istio
  annotations:
    description: "Permissive mTLS policy for backward compatibility during service mesh migration"
    migration-phase: "transitional"
spec:
  # Apply to all workloads in the microservices namespace
  mtls:
    # PERMISSIVE mode allows both plaintext and mTLS traffic
    # This ensures backward compatibility with services not yet enrolled in the mesh
    mode: PERMISSIVE
  # Optional: Override mTLS mode for specific ports on specific workloads
  # Uncomment and customize as needed during migration
  # selector:
  #   matchLabels:
  #     app: legacy-service
  # portLevelMtls:
  #   8080:
  #     mode: DISABLE
  #   8443:
  #     mode: STRICT
